name: Junior Mance
abstract: Julian Clifford Mance, Jr. (October 10, 1928 – January 17, 2021), known as Junior Mance, was an American jazz pianist and composer.

interviews.title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

concerts.label: WLIB Festival of Music and Drama
Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Junior Mance Trio

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: I work with Junior Mance and we have a trio and sometimes a quartet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Keter Betts
about_quotes.evidence: I met him in New York and we became friends... I went in for a month with him... Junior Mance went with Dinah for a while... And you’ve been working with Junior Mance recently? Yeah. On the cruise things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keter Betts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: The last one we did, “Blue Mance” well they gave us almost, we did it, we did it, we took the whole day... We have such a great rapport with Junior and Keter.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin Queen
about_quotes.evidence: Junior Mance I work with very often. I just did Junior’s new album... I produced two albums on Junior’s name... we did three tours of Japan together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alvin Queen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, August 23, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: So he hired me... The band was what I just named: him and Nat, Sam Jones, Junior Mance, and me. We made an album called Sharpshooters.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: And I--they had Junior Mance on the piano. Phew. And that was an experience. And JimmyCobb on the drums and myself, rhythm section.
